new_classes.label_en: Spirits obtained by distilling grape wine or grape marc (subheading 2208 20)
new_classes.definition_en: Spirits obtained by distillation of grape wine or grape marc, including wine distillate (raw distillate of wine) obtained by the primary distillation after alcoholic fermentation of grape wine. These products do not have the properties of neutral ethyl alcohol, nor of a finished spirit, and have an actual alcoholic strength by volume of less than 80% vol, undenatured.

new_classes.label_en: Distillation of grape wine or grape marc (production process for subheading 2208 20)
new_classes.label_de: Destillation von Wein oder Traubentrester (Herstellungsprozess für Unterposition 2208 20)
new_classes.definition_en: A distillation process in which grape wine or grape marc (pomace) is the raw material, including primary distillation after alcoholic fermentation of grape wine to produce wine distillate or raw distillate of wine.

coverage_explanation: The main distinguishing criteria are captured: (1) the product is a spirit obtained by distilling grape wine or grape marc, modelled via a specialised subclass of SpiritsBeverages2208 linked to a dedicated grape-wine/marc distillation process; (2) the alcoholic strength is less than 80% vol (decimalRange maxExclusive 80.0 on alcoholByVolumePercent); (3) the product is undenatured (isDenatured = false). A BFO-parthood restriction on the distillation process points to WineOfFreshGrapes2204 as a raw-material input, partially capturing the grape-wine origin. Coverage is reduced to ~0.82 because: (a) grape marc (pomace/trester) is not yet a named class in the TBox, so its role as an alternative raw material can only be described informally; (b) the legal note's exclusion of neutral ethyl alcohol character (a qualitative distinction between 'wine distillate/raw distillate' and a finished spirit) cannot be fully formalised without a dedicated NeutralAlcoholQuality complement class, which would risk over-commitment; these aspects are noted but not fully axiomatised.
